subject: Chemistry
options: Glucose is soluble in water because of having aldehyde functional group
Glucose remains in multiple isomeric form in its aqueous solution
Glucose is one of the monomer unit in sucrose
solution:
Glucose is soluble in water due to presence of alcohol functional group and extensive hydrogen bonding.
Glucose exist is open chain as well as cyclic forms in its aqueous solution.
Glucose having 6C atoms so it is hexose and having aldehyde functional group so it is aldose. Thus, aldohexose.
Glucose is monomer unit in sucrose with fructose.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Primary structure of proteins: Proteins may have one or more polypeptide chains. Each polypeptide in a protein has amino acids linked with each other in a specific sequence and it is this sequence of amino acids that is said to be the primary structure of that protein.

Secondary structure of proteins: The secondary structure of protein refers to the shape in which a long polypeptide chain can exist.

Tertiary structure of proteins: The tertiary structure of proteins represents overall folding of the polypeptide chains i.e., further folding of the secondary structure.

Quaternary structure of proteins: Some proteins are composed of two or more polypeptide chains referred to as subunits. The spatial arrangement of these subunits with respect to each other is known as quaternary structure.

During denaturation Quaternary, secondary and tertiary structures are destroyed, but primary structure remains intact.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
A tripeptide is a peptide consisting of three amino acids joined by peptide bonds. The exact nature and function of proteins is determined by the amino acids present and the order they occur.

 In these, an amine group from one residue forms an amide bond with the carboxyl group of a second residue, the amino group of the second forms an amide bond with the carboxyl group of the third. 

Let 3 different amino acid are A, B, C then following combination of tripeptides can be formed-

ABC, ACB, BAC, BCA, CAB, CBA

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Hydrogen bonding is responsible for the stacking of α-helix structure of protein.

The α-helix structure of proteins is stabilized by intramolecular H-bonding between C−O of one amino acid residue and the N−H of the fourth amino acid residue in the chain.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Step 1: The starting material is an aldohexose (like D-glucose). On prolonged heating with $HI$, it undergoes reduction to form n-hexane. This indicates that all six carbon atoms are linked in a straight chain.
$\text{CHO}-(\text{CHOH})_4-\text{CH}_2\text{OH} \xrightarrow{\text{HI, } \Delta} \text{CH}_3-\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}_2-\text{CH}_3$ (n-hexane)

Step 2: n-Hexane, when passed over heated $V_2O_5$ at $773 \text{ K}$ and $10-20 \text{ atm}$ pressure, undergoes aromatization (dehydrogenation and cyclization) to form benzene.
$\text{n-hexane} \xrightarrow{V_2O_5, \text{ 773 K, 10-20 atm}} \text{C}_6\text{H}_6$ (Benzene)

Step 3: Benzene reacts with benzoyl chloride ($\text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{COCl}$) in the presence of anhydrous $\text{AlCl}_3$ to form benzophenone. This is a Friedel-Crafts acylation reaction.
$\text{C}_6\text{H}_6 + \text{C}_6\text{H}_5\text{COCl} \xrightarrow{\text{Anhyd. AlCl}_3} \text{C}_6\text{H}_5-\text{CO}-\text{C}_6\text{H}_5$ (Benzophenone)

Step 4: Analyze the product (X), which is benzophenone.
The structure of benzophenone consists of two phenyl rings attached to a carbonyl group.
- Each phenyl ring contains $3$ $\pi$ bonds, so two rings have $6$ $\pi$ bonds.
- The carbonyl group ($\text{C=O}$) contains $1$ $\pi$ bond.
- Total number of $\pi$ bonds = $6 + 1 = 7$.
- Total number of $\pi$ electrons = $7 \times 2 = 14$.
- The oxygen atom in the carbonyl group has $2$ lone pairs of electrons.
- Total number of lone pair electrons = $2 \times 2 = 4$.

Total number of electrons in the $\pi$ bonds and lone pairs = $14 + 4 = 18$.

Answer: $18$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Amylopectin is insoluble in water and constitutes about 80-85% starch.

Amylopectin is a water-soluble polysaccharide and highly branched polymer of a-glucose units found in plants. It is one of the two components of starch, the other being amylose. Amylopectin bears a straight/linear chain along with a number of side chains which may be branched further.

solution:
Amylose is a polysaccharide used in various industries as a functional biomaterial. It is mainly a linear component consisting of about 100-10,000 glucose monomers linked by 1,4 alpha bindings. Amylose is a polymer that can be found in starch. It is a water-soluble component that accounts for 20 to 25% of starch. 

So, among the given two statements first statement is false and second statement is correct.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Glucose→∆NaHCO3no reaction

Glucose→∆HNO3saccharic acid

Glucose, on oxidation with nitric acid, gives saccharic acid.

Glucose→∆HIn-hexane

Glucose, on prolonged heating with hot HI, gives n-Hexane.

Glucose→∆Br2Gluconic acid

Glucose reacts with bromine water to form gluconic acid.

 Bromine water is a mild oxidising agent which selectively oxidizes aldehyde to carboxylic acid only. It doesn’t oxidise alcohol or ketone.

Hence option B is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
solution: Seliwanoff’s test is used to distinguish between glucose and fructose. Seliwanoff’s reagent (0.5% resorcinol in <math><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></math> <math><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi></math> ) gives red solution with fructose but no change in colour with glucose.

subject: Chemistry
solution: DNA and RNA are chiral molecules due to their structural components. Both contain pentose sugars: DNA contains deoxyribose and RNA contains ribose. These are D-sugars with multiple chiral centers (at $C_2$, $C_3$, and $C_4$ in the furanose ring). The D-designation comes from the stereochemical configuration at the chiral center furthest from the carbonyl carbon. The chiral centers in these sugar moieties are the primary source of chirality in nucleic acids, making them optically active molecules. While the phosphate units and bases also contribute to molecular structure, the fundamental chirality arises from the D-sugar component.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Seliwanoff reagent→ [Resorcinol+ Conc. HCl] 

Seliwanoff's test is a chemical test which distinguishes between aldose and ketose sugars. If the sugar contains a ketone group, it is a ketose. If a sugar contains an aldehyde group, it is an aldose.

 Ketoses show red colour with Seliwanoff Reagent.

solution:
A. Arginine and Tryptophan are essential amino acids - True
B. Histidine contains imidazole ring (heterocyclic ring with N) - Statement is False
C. Proline has a 5-membered pyrrolidine ring, not 6-membered - False
D. Glycine ($NH_2-CH_2-COOH$) has no chiral center (α-carbon has two H atoms) - True
E. Cysteine side chain is $-CH_2-SH$ (thiol). $MeS-CH_2-CH_2-$ is the side chain of Methionine, not Cysteine - False
Correct statements: A and D only

solution: The general trend for the activity for hydrogenation increases from left to right. In Hydrogenation reaction, catalytic activity depends on variable oxidation states as well as atomic size.
